title: Parsnip Soup with Orange and Ginger
description: A smooth parsnip soup with orange and ginger.
ingredients: Grated rind and juice of one orange
method: Peel and dice the parsnips and onion, and carrot if using.
Melt the butter in a large pan or pressure cooker base, then stir in the parsnips, onion, flour, ginger and orange rind.
Add the stock, mix well and bring to the boil.
Cook for about 20 minutes, or until the vegetables are soft.
Purée the soup, then pass through a sieve if you want a smoother texture.
Return to the pan, stir in the orange juice and reheat gently without boiling.
Add the cream if you like and season lightly.
notes: The full quantity of stock makes a fairly thin soup.
A carrot can be added for colour.

title: Cream of Mushroom Soup
description: A rich mushroom soup finished with sherry and cream.
ingredients: For the sauce: 1/4 tsp each nutmeg and white pepper
For the sauce: a good pinch of herb salt
For the sauce: 1 glass dry sherry
method: Melt the butter and add the onion. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the mushrooms and cover again for 5 minutes.
Add the water, stock cube, parsley and garlic, then set the mixture aside.
Using the same pan, add the butter, flour, milk, salt, nutmeg, pepper and herb salt.
Bring slowly to the boil, whisking all the time, then bubble for 5 minutes.
Stir in the mushroom mixture.
Cool in the fridge for 2 hours or overnight.
Bring back to a simmer, add extra seasoning if needed, then stir in the sherry and cream before serving.

title: Spanish Chargrilled Peppers
description: Chargrilled peppers with anchovies, olives and olive oil.
ingredients: 1/2 x 100g jar anchovy fillets in oil, drained and halved lengthways
method: Cut the peppers lengthways into quarters and remove the seeds.
Place the peppers skin-side up under a hot grill until the skins have charred and blackened.
Transfer immediately to a saucepan and cover with a lid.
When cool enough to handle, remove the skins.
Arrange the peppers in a shallow dish with the anchovies and olives between them.
Season with black pepper, scatter over the marjoram and pour over the olive oil.
Serve at room temperature.
notes: Good as a first course or as an accompaniment.

title: Sweet Pepper Pots
description: Soft peppers and tomatoes baked with cream, capers and Parmesan.
ingredients: 1 large red pepper, seeded and chopped
method: Melt the butter and add the pepper, onion and garlic. Cook gently, stirring often, until the peppers are soft.
Pour boiling water over the tomatoes, peel them, remove the seeds and chop the flesh.
Add the tomatoes to the pan and cook slowly for 15 minutes, until thick and soft.
Stir in the sugar, salt and a pinch of cayenne.
Spoon into small ovenproof dishes and leave to cool.
Rinse and chop the capers, then mix with the cream.
Spoon the cream mixture over the pepper base and sprinkle with Parmesan.
Bake in a very hot oven or grill until lightly browned.
notes: Fromage frais or crème fraîche can be used instead of some or all of the cream.

title: Salmon or Tuna Fish Cakes
description: Simple fish cakes made with mashed potato and parsley.
ingredients: 1/2lb / 200g canned salmon or tuna, or freshly cooked fish
method: Flake the fish and remove any skin and bones.
Combine the fish with the mashed potato, egg, onion, parsley and seasoning.
If the mixture is too soft, add 1 tbsp medium matzo meal.
If using freshly mashed potatoes, chill the mixture before shaping.
Form into 6–8 patties.
Dip in flour, then fry gently in shallow oil with a little butter or margarine until crisp and brown on both sides.
notes: If making salmon cakes, a little grated lemon rind can be added.

title: Shepherd’s Pie
description: Minced beef, vegetables, tomatoes and mashed potato baked with cheese.
ingredients: 15oz tin plum tomatoes or chopped plum tomatoes
method: Slice the onion and fry gently in margarine or oil with a little salt until soft.
Add the minced beef and fry until cooked.
Cook the mixed vegetables in a little boiling water until done, then drain.
Peel and boil the potatoes until soft, then mash with milk and margarine.
Season the meat with Worcestershire sauce and pepper, then mix in the vegetables.
Transfer the meat mixture to a dish and arrange the tomatoes on top.
Cover with the mashed potato and scatter with grated cheese.
Bake in a moderate oven until the top is golden.
notes: Crushed crisps can be added on top of the cheese for crunch.

title: Moroccan Chicken and Dried Fruit Tagine
description: Chicken baked with spices, prunes, apricots and toasted almonds.
ingredients: 15 fl oz chicken stock, or half stock and half white wine
method: If the fruit is not pre-soaked, cover with boiling water for 30 minutes, then drain.
Preheat the oven to 300°F / 150°C / Gas 2 and toast the almonds as it heats.
Remove the skin and visible fat from the chicken.
Put the cake meal, salt and pepper into a bag and shake the chicken pieces in it to coat lightly.
Brown the chicken in hot oil, then place in a lidded casserole and surround with the dried fruit.
In the same oil, gently sauté the chopped onion and garlic until golden.
Add the spices, stock and honey, bring to the boil and simmer for 5 minutes.
Stir in the potato flour mixed with lemon juice or water.
Pour over the chicken, cover and bake for about 1 hour, or until tender.
Garnish with the toasted almonds before serving.
notes: The recipe notes that lamb can also be used.

title: Lemon Pasta
description: Linguine with lemon, Parmesan, basil and rocket.
ingredients: Sea salt and freshly ground black pepper
method: Cook the pasta in plenty of boiling salted water for about 10 minutes, then drain well and return to the pan.
Beat the lemon juice and zest with the olive oil, then stir in the Parmesan until thick and creamy.
Season to taste.
Add the lemon sauce to the pasta and shake the pan so each strand is coated.
Finish by stirring in the chopped basil and rocket.

title: Roasted garlic potato mash
description: Creamy mashed potatoes with roasted garlic and crème fraîche.
ingredients: 2 heads of garlic, top third cut off
2 lb / 900g red-skinned potatoes, peeled and cut into chunks
method: Heat the oven to 200°C / 400°F / Gas 6.
Place the garlic on foil, drizzle with olive oil, wrap loosely and bake for 45–50 minutes until golden and soft.
Boil the potatoes in a large pot with the salt until soft, then drain and return to the pot.
Mash with the butter until smooth.
Add the crème fraîche and black pepper.
Squeeze the roasted garlic cloves out of their skins and mix gently into the potatoes.
Taste and adjust seasoning.
notes: Can be prepared earlier in the day and reheated gently.

title: Coldslaw
description: A cabbage and carrot slaw with mayonnaise and soured cream.
ingredients: Salt and freshly ground black pepper
method: Shred the cabbage and dry it well.
Mix together the vinegar, sugar, salt, pepper, mayonnaise and soured cream.
Add the cabbage, carrots and chives.
Toss lightly and adjust seasoning.
notes: The original notes mention optional steps to crisp the cabbage and marinate it first, but they can be skipped.

title: Chocolate Mousse I
description: A classic chocolate mousse with eggs and optional sherry or brandy.
method: Separate the eggs.
Beat the egg whites until stiff.
Melt the chocolate over hot water or in the microwave.
Add the egg yolks to the chocolate and beat well.
Add the sherry or brandy if using.
Fold in 1 tablespoon of the beaten egg whites, then fold in the rest gently.
Refrigerate until needed.

title: Lemon Cake
description: A simple lemon cake finished with a lemon icing syrup.
ingredients: For the syrup: 3 tbsp lemon juice
For the syrup: 3 tbsp icing sugar
method: Put all the cake ingredients into a bowl and beat until mixed.
Bake in a greased and floured 7½-inch round tin or loaf tin for about 1 hour at 350°F / 160°C / Gas 4.
When cold, prick the cake all over.
Warm the lemon juice and icing sugar together to make a syrup.
Pour the syrup over the cake.
